mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts signals a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline > 1.0% in consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a sustained rise in the federal funds rate increases the cost of capital and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples stabilize.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods do not yield positive 60-day forward returns with a win rate above 55%, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months, robust employment growth fuels consumer demand and earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return distribution in high-payroll months shows no significant upward bias (p > 0.10), or the signal generates false positives in > 40% of out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and decline in industrial production signals stagflationary pressure, which erodes real equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises and INDPRO falls in the same month
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS increase and INDPRO decline does not predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect reverses sign in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, rising CPI erodes real consumer purchasing power and compresses discretionary spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth stalls.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral when CPI exceeds 3.0% YoY, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
